title: Classify System by Comparing Slopes and Intercepts
description: Determine the number of solutions of a linear system by comparing slopes and y-intercepts after writing both equations in slope-intercept form.
condition: Two linear equations in two variables, rewritten or rewritable in the form y = mx + b.
response: If slopes differ → one solution; if slopes equal and intercepts differ → no solution; if slopes and intercepts equal → infinitely many solutions.
label: Principle / Model
example: Given $y = 3x - \tfrac{1}{3}$ and $3y = 9x - 1$ → rewrite second: $y = 3x - \tfrac{1}{3}$. Same slope and intercept ⇒ infinitely many solutions.
notes: Requires ability to convert to slope-intercept form when needed.

title: Identify Intersection on Graph
description: Locate the point where two graphed lines intersect to determine the solution to a system.
condition: Given a graph of two linear equations.
response: Read the ordered pair at the intersection point.
label: Production / Skill
example: If the lines intersect at $(2,3)$, then the solution is $(2,3)$.
notes: Requires accurate coordinate reading. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies]

title: Interpret Solution as Ordered Pair
description: Understand that a solution to a system is an ordered pair satisfying both equations.
condition: Given a system of two linear equations.
response: State the solution as an ordered pair $(x,y)$.
label: Principle / Model
example: If $x=1$ and $y=-2$ satisfy both equations, the solution is $(1,-2)$.

title: Isolate Variable Using Inverse Operations
description: Rearrange a linear equation to isolate one variable using inverse operations.
condition: Linear equation where a variable can be isolated (e.g., x = expression or ax + b = c).
response: Apply inverse operations to solve for the chosen variable.
label: Rule / Plan
example: From $x = 6 - 3y$, x is already isolated; from $3y = 2x + 4$, divide by 3 → $y = \tfrac{2}{3}x + \tfrac{4}{3}$.

title: Solve Linear Equation in One Variable
description: Isolate the variable in a linear equation using inverse operations.
condition: Given a linear equation in one variable (e.g., $ax+b=c$).
response: Apply inverse operations to isolate and solve for the variable.
label: Rule / Plan

title: Solve System by Substitution
description: Solve a system by substituting an expression for one variable into the other equation and solving.
condition: Given a system where one equation is solved for a variable or can be easily solved for one variable.
response: Substitute the expression into the other equation and solve for the remaining variable, then back‑substitute.
label: Principle / Model
example: Given $y=-4x+5$ and $5x+2y=-5$, substitute: $5x+2(-4x+5)=-5$ and solve for $x$.

title: Substitute Expression into Second Equation
description: Replace a variable in one equation with an equivalent expression from another equation.
condition: System of two equations where one variable is isolated or expressed in terms of the other.
response: Substitute the expression into the other equation and simplify to obtain an equation in one variable.
label: Rule / Plan
notes: Primary method used in Q1, Q3, and Q4.

title: Substitute Ordered Pair into Equation
description: Substitute given x- and y-values into a linear equation to evaluate or solve for a parameter.
condition: Given an equation and a specific ordered pair that satisfies it.
response: Replace x and y with the given values and simplify to find unknown parameters or verify truth.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Given solution $(6,-1)$ for $y=mx+3$, substitute: $-1=6m+3$ and solve for $m$.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Translate Context to System of Equations
description: Represent two real-world constraints involving two quantities as a system of linear equations.
condition: Word problem describes totals involving two unknown quantities.
response: Write two linear equations modeling the relationships.
label: Principle / Model
example: If photos are 4 more than twice quotes and total is 37, write $p = 2q + 4$ and $p + q = 37$.

title: Write Equation in Slope-Intercept Form
description: Express a linear equation in the form $y = mx + b$ using slope and a point.
condition: Slope and one point on a line are known.
response: Substitute into $y = mx + b$ and solve for $b$ to write the equation.
label: Principle / Model
example: With slope $-1$ and point $(-6,-1)$: $-1 = (-1)(-6) + b \Rightarrow -1 = 6 + b \Rightarrow b = -7$, so $y=-x-7$.
